title: The night the power went out in our building
summary: A short story about a blackout that brought neighbours together.
topic: The night the power went out in our building
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a small emergency in your home or building. Describe what you and others did. Use past simple, past continuous, and the connector 'when'.
hintStarters: One Wednesday evening in…, the lights suddenly went off…
After about ten minutes, my neighbour from across the hall…
sampleText: One Wednesday evening in Mexico City, the lights in our building suddenly went off just after eight. I was washing dishes at the kitchen sink, and the radio was playing a song I liked. The whole flat became silent and very dark. At first I thought I had blown a fuse, but when I looked out of the window, the entire street was black, including the small shop on the corner. I found a candle in the bottom drawer and lit it with one of the matches I keep for emergencies. After about ten minutes, my neighbour from across the hall knocked on my door. She was holding a torch and a bottle of cold beer. Other neighbours were already sitting on chairs in the corridor, talking in low voices. A boy from the third floor brought down a small radio that worked on batteries. We did not know any of them well, but in the soft light of three candles we spent an hour together. I learned that the woman next to me had moved into the building in 1992. The lights came back on at half past nine. We all clapped, then quietly went back to our quiet flats and our quiet evenings.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous + past simple with 'when'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Past continuous tả bối cảnh đang diễn ra ('I was washing dishes'), 'when + past simple' cho hành động đột ngột. Có thể đảo trật tự hai mệnh đề.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Past continuous sets the ongoing scene ('I was washing dishes'); 'when + past simple' adds the sudden interrupting event. The two clauses can swap order.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect: 'I had blown a fuse'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Had + V3' diễn tả điều xảy ra trước một mốc khác trong quá khứ. Ở đây: trước khi tôi nhận ra, tôi đã nghĩ rằng tôi *đã* làm nổ cầu chì.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Had + past participle' shows something that happened before another past moment. Here: before I checked outside, I thought I had blown a fuse.

sampleTranslationVi: Một tối thứ Tư ở Mexico City, đèn trong tòa nhà chúng tôi đột nhiên tắt phụt ngay sau tám giờ. Tôi đang rửa bát ở bồn rửa nhà bếp, và đài đang phát một bài hát tôi thích. Cả căn hộ trở nên im lặng và rất tối. Lúc đầu tôi nghĩ tôi đã làm nổ cầu chì, nhưng khi tôi nhìn ra cửa sổ, cả con phố tối đen, kể cả cửa hàng nhỏ ở góc phố. Tôi tìm thấy một cây nến trong ngăn kéo dưới cùng và thắp nó bằng một que diêm tôi cất sẵn để dùng khi khẩn cấp. Sau khoảng mười phút, người hàng xóm đối diện gõ cửa nhà tôi. Cô ấy cầm một cái đèn pin và một chai bia lạnh. Những người hàng xóm khác đã ngồi trên ghế ngoài hành lang, nói chuyện khe khẽ. Một cậu bé từ tầng ba mang xuống một chiếc đài nhỏ chạy bằng pin. Chúng tôi không thân ai trong số họ, nhưng trong ánh sáng dịu của ba ngọn nến, chúng tôi đã ở bên nhau một tiếng. Tôi biết được rằng người phụ nữ ngồi cạnh tôi đã chuyển vào tòa nhà từ năm 1992. Đèn sáng trở lại lúc chín giờ ba mươi. Tất cả chúng tôi vỗ tay, rồi lặng lẽ quay trở về căn hộ yên lặng của mình và buổi tối yên lặng của mình.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past-continuous + past-simple combination.
options: I washed dishes when the lights were going off.
I was washing dishes when the lights went off.
I was wash dishes when the lights went off.
I am washing dishes when the lights went off.
mcQuestions.explanation: Background = past continuous; interrupting event = past simple.

options: At first I thought I have blown a fuse.
At first I thought I had blown a fuse.
At first I thought I had blow a fuse.
At first I thought I was blown a fuse.
a temporary loss of electricity
a strong wind
an expensive electricity bill
a small fire in the kitchen
I lit the candle with a match.
I light the candle with a match yesterday.
I lighted the candle with a match yesterday.
I was lit the candle with a match.
a hand-held electric light
a small candle
an old radio
a kitchen stove

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural sentence with 'knock on'.
options: She knocked on my door.
She knocked at the my door.
She knocked door my.
She was knocking to my door.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Knock on (someone's) door' is the standard collocation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'talking ___ low voices.'
options: in
with
by
at
mcQuestions.explanation: 'In a low voice' / 'in low voices' = standard phrase.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past simple of 'come back on'.
options: The lights came back on at half past nine.
The lights come back on at half past nine.
The lights have come back on at half past nine.
The lights are coming back on at half past nine.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'came' fits the past event time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the meaning of 'blew a fuse'.
options: broke an electric fuse with too much current
lost a hat in strong wind
made a loud noise from a horn
burned a small candle
mcQuestions.explanation: To 'blow a fuse' = trip / break an electrical fuse, often by overloading.

options: We all clap, then quietly went back to our flats.
We all clapped, then quietly went back to our flats.
We all are clapping, then quietly going back to our flats.
We all clapping, then quietly went back to our flats.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple needed for both verbs in the story.

options: a long narrow passage inside a building
the kitchen of a flat
the lift in a building
a small balcony
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Corridor' = long passage connecting rooms or flats.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do small emergencies, like a power cut, sometimes bring neighbours together more than years of living next to each other?
